hero.title: Pastor
hero.subtitle: A life given to guiding people into clarity, truth, and alignment with God.

sections.text: My work in ministry is grounded in a long-standing commitment to leadership, teaching, and the formation of people over time.

sections.title: Formation
sections.text:
My leadership journey began within Scripture Union, where I served as Prayer Secretary during my secondary school years. It introduced me early to responsibility in spiritual life — not as performance, but as discipline.

That foundation developed further through the Ghana Fellowship of Evangelical Students (GHAFES), where I served as President of GHAFES-UCC and later as South-West Zone President. These experiences required consistency, accountability, and the ability to guide others while continuing to grow personally.

sections.title: Ministry
sections.text:
I currently serve as a Pastor at the Abundant Life Centre (Kwashieman Branch) of The Lord’s Pentecostal Church International. My work spans teaching, pastoral care, and leadership within the local church.

I also serve as the National Coordinator of TELPSAM (The Lord’s Pentecostal Students & Associates’ Ministry), where I lead and support the development of student ministry across different contexts.

I was formally ordained into ministry in 2024, a recognition of both the journey of formation and the responsibility to serve, lead, and teach.

sections.title: Focus
sections.text:
Much of my work centres on teaching, mentorship, and the long-term development of people.

I am particularly interested in helping individuals grow in clarity — in how they understand their faith, how they make decisions, and how they take responsibility for the lives they are building.

sections.title: Approach
sections.text:
My approach to ministry is shaped by a simple conviction:

Spiritual life cannot be separated from how people think, how they live, and how they build.

For that reason, I emphasise clarity, structure, and consistency — not as abstract ideals, but as practical foundations for growth.

I am less interested in momentary experiences, and more interested in whether a person’s life is being shaped in a way that will endure.

signature: To teach in a way that brings understanding.
To guide in a way that builds responsibility.
To contribute to the formation of people whose lives are grounded, aligned, and able to stand over time.
